idea-plugin.name: Gotchi
idea-plugin.description:
Raise a virtual pixel-art pet that lives inside your JetBrains IDE. Feed it, play with it, and keep it happy while you code — neglect it and it will get sick and eventually die.

Features

  • Persistent pet that survives IDE restarts with offline stat decay
  • 6 life stages: Egg → Baby → Child → Teen → Adult → Senior
  • 3 evolution tiers per stage (Best / Mid / Low) based on how well you care for your pet
  • Care quality score tracks hunger, happiness, discipline, cleanliness, and health over time
  • Coding activity rewards: your pet gets happier and more disciplined when you type
  • High score tracking: records your best run (age, stage, pet type) across all pets
  • Status bar widget showing your pet's current mood at a glance
  • Settings page — click the ⚙ gear icon in the Gotchi tool window, or go to Settings → Tools → Gotchi — to adjust font size, text colour, attention-call behaviour, and idle thresholds

Care Actions

  • Feed Meal — restores hunger (up to 3 meals per wake cycle)
  • Feed Snack — boosts happiness and hunger (up to 3 snacks per wake cycle; too many snacks cause sickness)
  • Play — raises happiness and burns energy (requires at least 25 energy)
  • Sleep / Wake — sleeping restores energy and heals HP faster; the pet auto-wakes when fully rested
  • Clean — removes droppings (too many droppings cause sickness)
  • Medicine — cures sickness after 3 doses
  • Scold / Praise — both raise discipline

Pet Types

  • Codeling — balanced all-rounder with moderate stats and a predictable poop schedule
  • Bytebug — fast hunger decay and very unpredictable bathroom habits; energetic energy regen
  • Pixelpup — happiness decays faster, needs frequent play; irregular poop timing
  • Shellscript — slow metabolism, high base health, near-clockwork poop schedule

Getting Started

Open the Gotchi tool window (right-hand panel) and click New Pet to hatch your first egg. Choose a pet type, pick a name, and start coding! Keep an eye on the status bar widget for a quick mood summary. Click the ⚙ gear icon in the Gotchi tool window title bar (or go to Settings → Tools → Gotchi) to adjust display size, colours, attention-call timing, and idle thresholds.
